data.title: Pakistan's Defense Minister: A new round of US-Iran talks will begin soon.
data.description: Pakistani Defense Minister Asif said in an interview that the prospect of resuming negotiations between the US and Iran remains, and a new round of talks will begin soon. (CCTV)

data.title: The American Bankers Association criticizes the White House's stablecoin report: its analysis is flawed and ignores the core risks of interest-bearing stablecoins.
data.description: The American Bankers Association (ABA) has publicly criticized the stablecoin report released by the White House Council of Economic Advisers (CEA), stating that its analysis is completely flawed. The CEA report argues that banning stablecoin yields would have little effect on protecting bank lending, increasing loan growth by only 0.02%, and actually harming consumers. However, the ABA counters that the report avoids the real risks: allowing stablecoins to pay interest would lead to a large-scale outflow of community bank deposits, increased financing costs, and tighter local credit. The ABA emphasizes that the CEA's focus on the "impact of the ban" creates a false sense of security; the rapid scaling up of interest-paying stablecoins is the far more destructive scenario.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Microsoft Develops New Copilot Features Inspired by OpenClaw
data.description: Microsoft has announced the development of new features for its Copilot program. According to Odaily, these enhancements are inspired by OpenClaw, a notable AI technology. The company aims to integrate advanced functionalities into Copilot, leveraging insights from OpenClaw's capabilities.

data.title: China Responds to Trump's Call for Oil Purchases
data.description: China's Foreign Ministry has issued a response following U.S. President Donald Trump's suggestion that China should purchase oil from the United States and Venezuela. According to NS3.AI, Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun emphasized that the primary focus should be on restoring peace and stability in the Gulf and Middle East regions as a fundamental step towards resolving the issue.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin retreats to $71,000 as renewed tensions in the Hormuz dampen risk appetite.
data.description: After the rally driven by the Middle East ceasefire subsided, Bitcoin hovered around $71,000, while Ethereum traded at approximately $2,190, with the overall crypto market weakening in tandem. Analysts point out that the breakdown in US-Iran negotiations and the escalating tensions surrounding the Strait of Hormuz have brought the market back to the macro trading logic of "rising oil prices—increased inflation expectations—declining risk appetite." Institutions believe that Bitcoin encountered significant resistance around $74,000, coupled with crude oil returning above $100, putting pressure on risk appetite. However, most opinions suggest that the current pullback has not yet evolved into panic selling. Data shows that the spot Bitcoin ETF still recorded a net inflow of nearly $1 billion last week, and the scale of forced liquidations was significantly lower than in the first quarter, indicating that the market's ability to absorb shocks has improved. Structurally, strong selling pressure remains in the $70,000 to $80,000 range, with approximately 13.5 million addresses in a floating loss state, limiting upside potential. Meanwhile, open interest in futures contracts has decreased by more than 50% from its 2025 peak, indicating that excessive leverage has been largely cleared and the market structure is becoming healthier. Bitcoin is currently behaving more like a macro asset than an independent market, and its price movements remain highly dependent on inflation and liquidity conditions. Given the resurgence of inflation in the US and the continued cautious monetary policy, Bitcoin is likely to maintain a volatile pattern in the short term. (The Block)

data.title: Glassnode: XRP derivatives leverage continues to contract, market sentiment remains cautious.
data.description: On-chain analytics platform Glassnode published an article on its X platform stating that after a sharp deleveraging in early October 2025, XRP perpetual contract open interest (OI) plummeted from 7 billion to 2 billion, a drop of 71%. Subsequently, market positions further compressed, with OI declining by approximately 25% to 1.5 billion. The market has not yet rebuilt speculative positions, indicating that derivatives trading participants as a whole remain cautious, and risk appetite has not yet significantly recovered.

data.title: Microsoft will develop new Copilot features inspired by OpenClaw.
data.description: Microsoft has announced that it is developing a new Copilot feature, reportedly inspired by OpenClaw. (Jinshi)

data.title: Foundry officially launched on the Zcash mining pool
data.description: Digital Currency Group (DCG)'s Foundry, a mining giant controlling approximately 31% of Bitcoin's hashrate, has officially launched a dedicated mining pool for Zcash (ZEC). The new pool offers compliant and auditable mining services to institutional and publicly traded miners. This move is seen as significant institutional endorsement for privacy coins, stemming from surging institutional interest in privacy assets. Foundry plans to apply its mature Bitcoin mining pool technology framework to the ZEC network, and it is expected that several publicly traded mining companies will allocate some of their hashrate to Zcash, potentially significantly improving the network's hashrate and security. (Fortune)

data.title: OFFICIAL TRUMP Token Sees Increased Purchases Ahead of Mar-a-Lago Event
data.description: Large holders of OFFICIAL TRUMP tokens have increased their purchases in anticipation of an April 25 luncheon at Mar-a-Lago. According to NS3.AI, the event invites the top 297 holders, with the top 29 receiving access to a private reception. Despite the increased buying activity, the token's price remains around $2.80, reflecting a decline of over 33% from $4.35 following U.S. President Donald Trump's March announcement of the event.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Adopts Cautious Bullish Stance as Market Risks Subside
data.description: On April 13, Morgan Stanley shifted to a cautiously bullish outlook, indicating that recent market risks have largely been absorbed. According to BlockBeats, strategist Michael Wilson noted that the S&P 500 index has rebounded approximately 7% from its low and maintained key support levels, suggesting that a market correction may be nearing its end.

data.title: Hungarian Election Results May Improve EU Relations, ING Analysts Say
data.description: On April 13, analysts from ING released a report highlighting the unexpected strength of the mandate for change following Hungary's recent elections. According to Jin10, the analysts noted that the two-thirds majority secured by the new government reduces short-term policy uncertainty and raises expectations for institutional reforms, potentially improving relations with the European Union and enhancing fiscal credibility faster than anticipated. However, they cautioned that structural changes might require more than one political term to fully implement. Despite potential short-term fiscal deterioration due to the abolition of legacy budgets, the analysts believe that the long-term benefits could convince market participants to allow the new government the necessary time to reshape the nation.

data.title: Foundry's Zcash Mining Pool Gains Significant Network Share
data.description: Foundry has launched a Zcash mining pool and block explorer, quickly capturing approximately 30% of the network's hashrate. According to NS3.AI, the pool attracted multiple institutional miners prior to its public debut, following an initial announcement in March. The pool is now open to new institutional participants, with a focus on onboarding regulated entities.

data.title: Hyperliquid Founder Jeffrey Yan's Journey from Harvard to Cryptocurrency
data.description: Jeffrey Yan, the founder of Hyperliquid, has been profiled by Dom Cooke, highlighting his academic journey and career in cryptocurrency. According to NS3.AI, Yan's early education laid the foundation for his studies at Harvard, where he developed an interest in digital currencies. His passion led to the establishment of Hyperliquid, a company focused on cryptocurrency innovations. Yan currently resides in Singapore, where he continues to advance his work in the crypto sector.

data.title: Strategy spent a staggering $1 billion to increase its Bitcoin holdings in a single week, a 203% surge compared to the previous week.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, as of 8:00 AM ET on April 13, 2026, global listed companies (excluding mining companies) saw a net purchase of $1 billion in Bitcoin last week, a 36% increase compared to the previous week. Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) announced an investment of $1 billion (a 203% increase compared to the previous week) to purchase 13,927 Bitcoins at a price of $71,902, bringing its total holdings to 780,897 Bitcoins. Japanese listed company Metaplanet did not purchase any Bitcoins last week. In addition, one other company purchased Bitcoins last week. A French Bitcoin company announced on April 13 that it invested $2.6 million to purchase 37 Bitcoins at a price of $70,168.90, bringing its total holdings to 2,925 Bitcoins. As of press time, the total number of Bitcoins held by listed companies worldwide (excluding mining companies) is 1,047,244, an increase of 1.35% compared to last week, with a current market value of approximately US$74.29 billion, accounting for 5.2% of Bitcoin's circulating market capitalization.

data.title: Putin Highlights Economic Relations with Indonesia Amid Trade Decline
data.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin emphasized the strengthening economic relationship between Russia and Indonesia during discussions at the Kremlin with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto. Bloomberg posted on X that despite the positive outlook, Putin acknowledged a recent decrease in trade between the two nations. The meeting focused on enhancing bilateral cooperation and exploring opportunities for future growth in various sectors.

data.title: WisdomTree saw net inflows of $98 million into digital assets in the first quarter, bringing its total assets under management to $867 million.
data.description: Asset management firm WisdomTree released its March monthly metrics report, revealing that despite the sluggish cryptocurrency market, its digital assets saw net inflows of $98 million in the first quarter of this year, thanks to the launch of tokenized money market funds. As of March 31, 2026, its digital asset management assets reached a record $867 million. (Businesswire)

data.title: Strategy currently has unrealized losses of approximately $3.61 billion, while Bitmine has unrealized losses of approximately $7.004 billion.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Bitcoin treasury company Strategy (MSTR) purchased 13,927 BTC ($1 billion) last week at approximately $71,902 per BTC. They now hold a total of 780,897 BTC ($55.408 billion), with an average cost of $75,577, resulting in a paper loss of $3.61 billion (-6.1%). Ethereum treasury company Bitmine (BMNR) purchased 71,524 ETH ($155 million) last week at approximately $2,176 per ETH. They now hold a total of 4,874,858 ETH ($10.656 billion), with an average cost of $3,623, resulting in a paper loss of $7.004 billion (-39.6%).

data.title: Iran's acting defense minister: Iranian armed forces are on the highest level of alert.
data.description: According to Iran's Tasnim News Agency on the 13th, Iran's acting Defense Minister Ibn Reza stated that the Iranian armed forces are currently on the highest level of alert and are fully prepared for all possibilities. He emphasized that any hostile actions will be met with a "strong and decisive response," making the other side pay the price for its aggression and regret it. (Xinhua)

data.title: In an interview, Trump called on China to buy oil from the United States and Venezuela.
data.description: On April 13, Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun hosted a regular press conference. A reporter asked about Trump's interview call for China to buy US and Venezuelan oil. Guo Jiakun stated that regarding the issue of oil purchases, China is willing to work with all parties to safeguard global energy security and the stability of supply chains. However, to fundamentally resolve the issue, the first priority should be to promote the restoration of peace and stability in the Gulf and the Middle East. Venezuela is a sovereign state with full and permanent sovereignty over its natural resources and all economic activities, and has the right to independently choose its partners; other countries have no right to interfere. (Foreign Ministry)

data.title: Bitmine increased its ETH holdings by 71,524 last week, bringing its total staked ETH to over 3.33 million.
data.description: Ethereum treasury firm Bitmine Immersion Technologies disclosed that it increased its holdings of 71,524 ETH last week. Its current cryptocurrency holdings include 4,874,858 ETH, 198 BTC, $85 million worth of Eightco Holdings equity, and $200 million worth of Beast Industries shares. In addition, the company has a total of 3,334,637 ETH staked (equivalent to $7.4 billion at $2,206 per ETH). (PRNewswire)
